prompt: Run sdlc-test in SYSTEM mode (/sdlc:test) against the complete docs/ chain in this project (PRD.yaml, DATA-MODEL.yaml, ARCH.yaml, plus API.yaml + API__* and UX.yaml + UX__* enrichers — all status: complete). For every multiple-choice question pick the recommended (position-1) option; for free-text give a one-line plausible answer; accept inferred pre-fills as proposed; skip optional themes at the gate. Then write docs/TEST-STRATEGY.yaml and run validate_schema.py until it validates. The product is a small web app task tracker (tinytrack) with a weekly digest. KEY CANARIES: (1) Workflow coverage is GATED — WKF-001 (create/triage a task), WKF-002 (comment + close), and WKF-003 (configure digest + review runs) are each traced by >=2 ARCH containers (web-frontend + backend-api; WKF-003 also digest-worker), so each MUST be covered by at least one system test (e2e or contract) whose `covers` names it, OR deferred via a WRN-NNN. The validator HARD-fails on an uncovered cross-container workflow. (2) The system file carries the GLOBAL policy every container inherits: test_approach.pyramid_targets, coverage_threshold.line_pct, mock_policy and fixture_strategy are all required. (3) Every test is a unique TST-NNN item with non-empty directives, a `covers` list of FR/NFR/ACR/WKF ids that resolve to PRD, `involves_containers` that resolve to ARCH container_ids, a priority and an acceptance line. Do NOT author per-container unit tests here — those belong in container mode.

assertions.text: docs/TEST-STRATEGY.yaml exists and can be parsed as valid YAML
metadata.status == 'complete'
metadata.changelog contains at least one entry
test_approach.pyramid_targets is present and non-empty
coverage_threshold.line_pct is present and is a number
mock_policy is present and non-empty
fixture_strategy is present and non-empty
Every tst_id in tests[] matches the pattern TST-\d{3,} and is unique across the file
Every test item has a non-empty directives list (at least one directive string)
Every test item has a non-empty covers list
Every entry in every test's covers[] is a recognizable PRD id (matches FR-\d+, NFR-\d+, WKF-\d+, ACR-\d+, or JTB-\d+)
Every entry in every test's involves_containers[] matches a container_id that exists in ARCH.yaml containers[]
Every test item has a non-empty priority field
Every test item has a non-empty acceptance field
WKF-001 appears in at least one test's covers[] OR is deferred via a WRN-NNN entry in test_strategy_warnings[]
WKF-002 appears in at least one test's covers[] OR is deferred via a WRN-NNN entry in test_strategy_warnings[]
WKF-003 appears in at least one test's covers[] OR is deferred via a WRN-NNN entry in test_strategy_warnings[]
Every entry in test_strategy_warnings[] matches the pattern ^WRN-\d{3,}:\s+.+
No test in the system file is tier 'unit' (unit tests belong in container mode, not system mode)
validate_schema.py exits with code 0 when run against docs/TEST-STRATEGY.yaml

The prd_warnings WRN-001 (digest idempotency / double-send risk on FR-005) is addressed: either a system-level test's covers[] includes FR-005 or a note about double-send, OR a WRN-NNN deferral in test_strategy_warnings[] references the idempotency / double-send risk

The PRD edge case EDG-001 (digest run with zero open tasks) is addressed: either a test's description or acceptance references the zero-task scenario, OR a WRN-NNN deferral in test_strategy_warnings[] names EDG-001 or the zero-open-tasks condition
The system tests[] list contains more than one test (the suite is not collapsed to a single test covering everything)
WKF-001, WKF-002, and WKF-003 are each covered by distinct tests (no single test is the only entry covering all three workflows at once)
At least one test uses tier 'e2e' (cross-container workflow tests warrant e2e tier)
NFR-001 (p95 task-list latency) is addressed by at least one test whose covers[] includes NFR-001, or deferred via a WRN-NNN
